theorem_candidates.name: family_section_theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: If A1 and A2 hold, stable concept identity is better modeled as family-conditioned local sections than as globally uniform points.

theorem_candidates.name: restricted_readout_transport_theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: If A5 and A6 hold, discriminative readout can only remain stable on restricted overlaps and admissible paths, not by direct global collapse.

theorem_candidates.name: stress_guarded_update_theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: If A3 and A4 hold, novelty-heavy updates must contract into guarded-write regimes before stable-read collapses.

theorem_candidates.name: anchored_bridge_lift_theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: If A1, A3, and A6 hold, relation-role structure should emerge as a family-anchored lift rather than a free symbolic layer.

transport_law.formal: Tau_total(c, mode_1 -> mode_2) = Tau_read^(f_c) + Phi(mode_1 -> mode_2) + Psi_reason^(f_c) - switch_cost(c) - fragility(c)

components.switch_cost(c): mode transition cost for concept c
components.fragility(c): transport fragility under stress and overlap limits

verdict.core_answer: ICSPB has now advanced from axiom and operator levels into a first theorem / exclusion / transport-law layer.
verdict.next_theory_target: bind these theorem candidates to brain-side falsification and filtered engineering benchmarks.
